name: Senda de la Dama Roja Trail
name_original: Cuevas de Covalanas y Cullalvera – circular desde Ramales de la Victoria por el Monte Pando
description: Hike a moderate 6.3-mile loop from Ramales de la Victoria to explore the prehistoric caves of Covalanas and Cullalvera, gaining 1421 feet.
region_intro: Hiking around Ramales De La Victoria offers diverse terrain characterized by intensely eroded limestone mountains, deep river valleys, and extensive cave systems. The region is shaped by the Asón, Gándara, and Carranza rivers, which create picturesque landscapes and waterfalls. Hikers can explore a rich natural environment featuring prominent peaks like Pico San Vicente and numerous prehistoric caves. This area provides a variety of routes suitable for different skill levels, from riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails in Ramales De La Victoria The most popular hiking route is Senda de la Dama Roja Trail, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This loop explores the area's renowned prehistoric caves, including Covalanas and Cullalvera. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico de San Vicente loop from Ramales de la Victoria, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic views from Pico San Vicente. Local hikers also love the Cueva de Cullalvera – circular from Camping La Barguilla by the river Asón, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through the scenic Asón river valley, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking around Ramales De La Victoria is defined by limestone mountains, river valleys, and cave systems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ramales De La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ramales De La Victoria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ramales De La Victoria?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails in Ramales De La Victoria, offering a variety of experiences. You'll find routes ranging from easy riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Ramales De La Victoria?
A: The region is characterized by intensely eroded limestone mountains, deep river valleys, and extensive cave systems. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, from lush riverbanks along the Asón, Gándara, and Carranza rivers to impressive peaks like Pico San Vicente.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ramales De La Victoria?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001205514/cueva-de-cullalvera-circular-from-camping-la-barguilla-by-the-river-ason" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Cullalvera – circular from Camping La Barguilla by the river Asón&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.9 km route along the scenic Asón river valley, perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: Can I find circular walks in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Ramales De La Victoria are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001216944/senda-de-la-dama-roja-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Dama Roja Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.2 km loop that explores the prehistoric caves of Covalanas and Cullalvera.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while hiking?
A: Ramales De La Victoria is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore impressive limestone mountains, picturesque river valleys, and significant prehistoric caves. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a stunning 70-meter cascade, or the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cullalvera Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4116860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalanas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views of the valley.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, the region offers difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de San Vicente loop from Ramales de la Victoria&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.6 km path that leads to panoramic views from Pico San Vicente (957m).

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ramales De La Victoria?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural features like caves and waterfalls, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails?
A: While many trails in natural areas of Cantabria are generally dog-friendly, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock. Specific regulations for individual trails or nature reserves should be checked locally.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with scenic vistas?
A: Yes, the region boasts several viewpoints offering spectacular panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4116860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalanas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is particularly well-known, providing extensive views of the valley and surrounding mountains, complete with information panels and picnic facilities.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads?
A: While Ramales De La Victoria is accessible by public transport, reaching specific trailheads often requires a car. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for connections to the town, and then plan for local transport or driving to the starting points of the hikes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the famous caves of the region?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its prehistoric caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001216944/senda-de-la-dama-roja-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Dama Roja Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate loop that specifically explores the area's famous prehistoric caves, including Covalanas and Cullalvera, offering a unique blend of hiking and cultural exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ramales De La Victoria?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Spring brings lush greenery and full waterfalls, while autumn showcases beautiful fall colors. Summer can be warm, but higher altitude trails provide cooler options. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions, especially in the mountains.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is designed to be completed in approximately 3 hours and 16 minutes, making it a good option for a half-day adventure.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate, suitable for hikers with good physical fitness and requiring sure-footedness. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved paths, some paved sections, and short street portions. The ascent involves narrow paths through diverse forests, and the broader area features a karstic system with limestone cliffs and dense holm oak groves.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural and historical highlights. You'll pass by several caves, including the entrances to Cullalvera Cave, Cueva del Agua, Cueva Baranda, and Covalanas Cave. You'll also reach the Peña Pando Summit and Bunker, and enjoy views from the Covalanas Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Are the Covalanas and Cullalvera caves accessible directly from the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes by the entrances of these significant caves, visiting their interiors typically requires separate, guided tours and advance booking. The Covalanas Cave is a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its Paleolithic rock art, and Cullalvera Cave is a massive karstic system.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is generally dog-friendly, but always ensure your dog is on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near cave entrances. Be mindful that dogs may not be permitted inside the actual caves if you plan to take a guided tour.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the hiking trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific trail. However, as mentioned, guided tours to enter the Covalanas and Cullalvera caves require separate booking and payment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, but the forested sections provide shade. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for potentially cooler and wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Ramales de la Victoria. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as the trailhead for this and other local routes. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta de Carlos V, the [CIMA CA07] Los Tornos * Ramales de la Victoria, and the Camino de los Carlistas.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach Ramales de la Victoria?
faqs.answer: Ramales de la Victoria is accessible by public transport, with bus services connecting it to larger towns and cities in Cantabria, such as Santander, and potentially to Bilbao. Checking local bus schedules is recommended for planning your journey to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers magnificent views of the valley and surrounding mountains, including the prominent Pico San Vicente, especially from viewpoints like the Covalanas Viewpoint. The natural environment is dominated by the geological formations of Monte Pando and lush Cantabrian holm oak forests.
